title: श्री हनुमान आरती
devanagariText:
॥ श्री हनुमान जी की आरती ॥

आरती कीजै हनुमान लला की।
दुष्ट दलन रघुनाथ कला की॥

जाके बल से गिरिवर काँपै।
रोग दोष जाके निकट न झाँके॥
आरती कीजै हनुमान लला की॥

अंजनि पुत्र महा बलदाई।
संतन के प्रभु सदा सहाई॥
आरती कीजै हनुमान लला की॥

दे बीरा रघुनाथ पठाए।
लंका जारि सिया सुधि लाए॥
आरती कीजै हनुमान लला की॥

लंका सो कोट समुद्र सी खाई।
जात पवनसुत बार न लाई॥
आरती कीजै हनुमान लला की॥

लंका जारि असुर सब मारे।
सियारामजी के काज सँवारे॥
आरती कीजै हनुमान लला की॥

लक्ष्मण मूर्छित पड़े सकारे।
आणि संजीवन प्राण उबारे॥
आरती कीजै हनुमान लला की॥

पैठि पाताल तोरि जम कारे।
अहिरावण की भुजा उखारे॥
आरती कीजै हनुमान लला की॥

बायें भुजा असुर दल मारे।
दाहिने भुजा संतजन तारे॥
आरती कीजै हनुमान लला की॥

सुर नर मुनि आरती उतारें।
जय जय जय हनुमान उचारें॥
आरती कीजै हनुमान लला की॥

कंचन थार कपूर लौ छाई।
आरती करत अंजना माई॥
आरती कीजै हनुमान लला की॥

जो हनुमान जी की आरती गावै।
बसि बैकुंठ परम पद पावै॥
आरती कीजै हनुमान लला की॥
दुष्ट दलन रघुनाथ कला की॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Hanuman Ji Ki Aarti ||

Aarti Keejai Hanuman Lala Ki.
Dusht Dalan Raghunaath Kala Ki.

Jaake Bal Se Girivar Kaanpai.
Rog Dosh Jaake Nikat Na Jhaanke.
Aarti Keejai Hanuman Lala Ki.

Anjani Putra Maha Baldaai.
Santan Ke Prabhu Sada Sahaai.
Aarti Keejai Hanuman Lala Ki.

De Beera Raghunaath Pathaaye.
Lanka Jaari Siya Sudhi Laaye.
Aarti Keejai Hanuman Lala Ki.

Lanka So Kot Samudra Si Khaai.
Jaat Pavansut Baar Na Laai.
Aarti Keejai Hanuman Lala Ki.

Lanka Jaari Asur Sab Maare.
Siyaraamji Ke Kaaj Sanvaare.
Aarti Keejai Hanuman Lala Ki.

Lakshman Moorchit Pade Sakaare.
Aani Sanjeevan Praan Ubaare.
Aarti Keejai Hanuman Lala Ki.

Paithi Paataal Tori Jam Kaare.
Ahiraavan Ki Bhuja Ukhaare.
Aarti Keejai Hanuman Lala Ki.

Baayein Bhuja Asur Dal Maare.
Daahiney Bhuja Santjan Taare.
Aarti Keejai Hanuman Lala Ki.

Sur Nar Muni Aarti Utaarein.
Jai Jai Jai Hanuman Uchaarein.
Aarti Keejai Hanuman Lala Ki.

Kanchan Thaar Kapoor Lau Chhaai.
Aarti Karat Anjana Maai.
Aarti Keejai Hanuman Lala Ki.

Jo Hanuman Ji Ki Aarti Gaavai.
Basi Baikunth Param Pad Paavai.
Aarti Keejai Hanuman Lala Ki.
Dusht Dalan Raghunaath Kala Ki.
hindiMeaning: यह श्री हनुमान जी की प्रसिद्ध आरती है। इसमें हनुमान जी की स्तुति करते हुए कहा गया है कि वे दुष्टों का दलन करने वाले और रघुनाथ जी की कला हैं। उनके बल से पर्वत काँपते हैं और उनके पास रोग-दोष नहीं आते। वे अंजनी पुत्र हैं, संतों के सहायक हैं। उन्होंने लंका जलाई, सीता माता की सुधि लाई, संजीवनी लाकर लक्ष्मण के प्राण बचाए, और अहिरावण का वध किया। जो यह आरती गाता है वह बैकुंठ में परम पद पाता है।
benefits: Invokes the blessing of the complete blessings of lord hanuman
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of diseases and negative influences
Invokes spiritual elevation and divine grace
Protects the household from evil forces
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of righteous desires of the devotee
Leads to attainment of Vaikuntha (divine abode)
description: The Hanuman Aarti, known in Sanskrit as श्री हनुमान आरती, is a traditional worship song (aarti) in praise of Lord Hanuman. This revered text originates from Traditional. Devotees recite this prayer to protect the household from evil forces and grant spiritual elevation and divine grace. It is also said to fulfill all righteous desires of the devotee. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Hanuman Jayanti. The text contains 11 verses and takes around 5 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Hanuman Aarti in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Hanuman Aarti - Aarti Keeje Hanuman Lala Ki in Hindi with English transliteration. The most popular aarti of Lord Hanuman.
relatedSearches: aarti kije hanuman lala ki lyrics
aarti kije hanuman lala ki dushtan dalan ram dulare

seoFaqs.question: What does 'Aarti Kije Hanuman Lala Ki' mean line by line?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening line — 'Aarti kije Hanuman lala ki, dushta dalan Raghunath kala ki' — means: 'Let us perform the aarti of beloved Hanuman, the destroyer of the wicked, the very form of Lord Raghunath (Rama).' Subsequent stanzas honour Hanuman as Anjani's brave son who flew across the ocean to Lanka, who burned the city of Ravana, who carried the Sanjivani mountain to revive an unconscious Lakshmana, and at whose name evil spirits flee. The aarti closes by saying that whoever sings it with devotion is granted residence in Vaikuntha and attains the supreme spiritual state. The text is a compact summary of Hanuman's most celebrated Ramayana exploits. (Sources: 108Puzzles; Bhakti Marg)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Hanuman Aarti be performed during worship?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditionally the Hanuman Aarti is performed at the conclusion of any Hanuman puja — after the Hanuman Chalisa, Sundara Kanda, or Bajrang Baan recitation — as the final offering of light to the deity. In daily home worship, it is usually performed twice: once at sunrise (Mangala Aarti) after bathing the murti and offering flowers, and once at sunset (Sandhya Aarti) with a lit ghee or camphor lamp. On Tuesdays and Saturdays the evening aarti is performed with extra reverence, often after offering boondi, laddu, or chola (vermilion paste) to the deity. The aarti is sung while rotating a five-wick lamp clockwise before the murti, and devotees take the lamp's warmth with their hands and touch their eyes at the end. (Source: Drik Panchang)

seoFaqs.question: Who is mentioned as performing Hanuman's aarti in the song?
seoFaqs.answer: A distinctive verse describes Anjani Mata — Hanuman's mother — performing his aarti on a golden plate (suvaran thar) lit with camphor (kapoor): 'Suvaran thar kapoor ki bati / Aarti karat Anjani mata.' This image is theologically rich: the divine son who is worshipped by millions is, in this stanza, lovingly aartied by his own mother, reminding devotees that Hanuman is the eternally devoted child of Anjana. Many bhajan traditions in Maharashtra and North India elaborate this image in separate compositions on Anjani Mata. The verse also reinforces the belief that worship offered with a mother's love is the highest form of aarti. (Source: 108Puzzles)

seoFaqs.question: What are the rewards promised in the closing verse of the Hanuman Aarti?
seoFaqs.answer: The concluding stanza promises that anyone who sings or hears Hanuman's aarti with devotion will dwell in Baikunth (Vaikuntha, the celestial abode of Vishnu) and attain the highest spiritual state — 'Jo Hanumanji ki aarti gave / Basi Baikunth param pad pave.' This phala-shruti (declaration of fruits) is a standard feature of Hindu aartis but is especially significant here because it links Hanuman-bhakti directly to Vishnu-loka, reinforcing Hanuman's identity as the foremost servant of Rama (an avatara of Vishnu). Devotees recite this closing line as a sankalpa, a vow that their devotion be accepted. (Source: My Pooja Box)

seoFaqs.question: Is the Hanuman Aarti part of a longer Aarti Sangrah?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. 'Aarti Kije Hanuman Lala Ki' is the most popular Hanuman aarti, but several traditional Aarti Sangrahs (anthologies) also include shorter or regional Hanuman aartis such as 'Aarti Shri Hanumanta' and 'Jai Hanuman Maharudra' from the Maharashtrian tradition. In a typical Hanuman Mandir, the priest may recite two or three aartis in sequence — beginning with the universal Aarti Kije Hanuman Lala Ki, followed by a regional or sampradaya-specific aarti. Many printed pothi books (such as the popular Gita Press Hanumat Upasana) place the Hanuman Aarti directly after the Hanuman Chalisa, the Bajrang Baan, and the Sankat Mochan Hanuman Ashtak, forming a complete daily Hanuman path. (Source: Sanatan Pathshala)
